key_themes: Turnaround to positive cash flow with strong balance sheet and cost savings
Clinical/guideline support for IPE but persistent revenue pressure from generics
Analyst caution on valuation/growth with PT below current price
sources_checked: Seeking Alpha articles and earnings transcripts
summary: Amarin has delivered clear operational progress by achieving positive cash flow in Q4 2025 (ahead of plan), reaching ~$303M cash with no debt, sustaining >50% U.S. IPE market share, completing its international partner transition, and realizing roughly half of $70M in OPEX savings. New REDUCE-IT data and updated 2026 ACC/AHA guidelines reinforce the science, supporting moderate optimism. However, revenues continue to decline (Q4 2025 ~$49M, -21% Y/Y; full-year ~$212-217M), restructuring costs came in slightly higher, and analyst consensus is Hold with a $12 average PT (vs. ~$15 current price), reflecting skepticism on sustainable growth. Biggest risk is ongoing revenue erosion if international partners (e.g. Recordati) under-deliver or no meaningful BD/acquisition materializes.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/AMRN/press-releases/)[[2]](https://www.biospace.com/press-releases/amarin-announces-preliminary-2025-financial-highlights-and-operational-accomplishments-including-achieving-positive-cash-flow-company-well-positioned-to-capture-global-growth-opportunities)[[3]](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/amarin-announces-preliminary-2025-financial-120000677.html)

recent_posts.highlight: Referenced past AMRN hype cycle (stock from $20+ to $1 with reverse split and CEO change) as cautionary tale against over-optimism on catalysts.
Preliminary 2025 results highlighted positive cash flow ahead of schedule, U.S. leadership, managed care wins, $70M savings on track, and positioning for 2026 global opportunities.

Multiple articles titled 'Amarin Is A Sell Despite the Large Cash Balance,' 'Headwinds Persist,' and 'Small Signs of Recovery May Not Be Enough'; some note hope from activism (Sarissa) or international pivot but emphasize topline contraction.
